blocks.body: Sentence Transformations — Reported Speech. Recast statements, questions, and commands into reported speech without losing time or speaker roles.

{"level":"B1-B2","intro":"In reported-speech transformations, you change more than the verb form. You also need to control backshift, pronouns, word order, time expressions, and the type of utterance.","rules":[{"label":"STATEMENT, QUESTION, COMMAND","color":"emerald","text":"First decide whether the original sentence is a statement, a question, or a command / request. The pattern depends on that choice.","example":"said that / asked if / told me to"},{"label":"BACKSHIFT WHEN NEEDED","color":"blue","text":"When the reporting verb is in the past, backshift often appears, but not mechanically in every case.","example":"am -> was, have done -> had done"},{"label":"CHANGE THE FRAME","color":"amber","text":"Together with the tense, you often need to change pronouns, time expressions, and the word order of questions.","example":"tomorrow -> the next day, Where are you? -> where I was"}]}

blocks.heading: 1. How to Spot the Needed Rewrite
blocks.body:
In reported-speech transformation tasks, start with the utterance type and the supporting changes it triggers.

  • Statement -> most often said (that) or told + object + (that).
  • Question -> asked, wanted to know, or wondered; in a reported question, you return to statement word order.
  • Command / request -> usually told / asked + object + to-infinitive.
  • Then check backshift, pronoun shift, and changes such as today -> that day and here -> there.

blocks.heading: 2. Core Transformation Patterns
blocks.body:
Base sentenceTarget sentenceRule focus
Anna said, “I can't stay long.”Anna said that she couldn't stay long.statement + backshift + pronoun change.
He asked me, “Where do you work?”He asked me where I worked.reported question with no inversion after asked.
The teacher said, “Please close the window.”The teacher told us to close the window.request / command -> told + object + to-infinitive.
They said, “We are meeting tomorrow.”They said that they were meeting the next day.time expression shift together with backshift.

In exam tasks, reported questions, commands, and short time-word shifts are especially common.

{"title":"3. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Keeping inversion in a reported question.","wrong":"He asked where did I work.","right":"He asked where I worked.","hint":"After asked, the indirect question returns to normal statement order."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Leaving out the object before the to-infinitive in commands and requests.","wrong":"She told to sit down.","right":"She told me to sit down.","hint":"Tell usually needs the person who receives the instruction."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Applying backshift mechanically even when the fact is still generally true.","wrong":"The teacher said that water boiled at 100°C.","right":"The teacher said that water boils at 100°C.","hint":"General truths often keep the present form."},{"label":"Mistake 4","color":"rose","title":"Forgetting pronoun and time-word changes.","wrong":"She said that I would finish this tomorrow.","right":"She said that she would finish it the next day.","hint":"Always check who is speaking, who is listening, and when the message is being reported."}]}

{"title":"4. Quick Exam Check","items":["Start by identifying the utterance type: statement, question, or command.","In reported questions, return to statement word order.","In commands and requests, check the pattern told / asked + object + to-infinitive.","After rewriting, confirm the backshift, pronouns, time expressions, and overall meaning."]}
